task_completion.description: Select the solo 'Memory game with images' activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks on the icon for the 'Memory game with images'. The selected game must NOT have the Tux penguin icon indicating the 'against Tux' mode.

task_completion.description: The memory game board is successfully loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A game board with a grid of face-down cards is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The agent interacts with the game by flipping cards.
task_completion.visual_evidence: One or two cards are shown face-up, revealing images (e.g., animals, objects) while the rest remain face-down.

task_completion.description: The agent successfully matches pairs of images.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Pairs of matching cards are revealed and either remain face-up or are removed from the board, indicating progress in the game.

task_completion.description: The agent completes the memory game.
task_completion.visual_evidence: All cards are successfully matched, and a completion/victory animation (such as a smiling flower, glowing stars, or a congratulatory message typical of GCompris) is displayed.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GCompris graphical interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse cursor is visible clicking on cards and menus within the GCompris application window. No terminal commands were used to bypass the game.

integrity.description: The agent played the single-player version, not 'against Tux'.
integrity.visual_evidence: Tux the penguin is NOT visible on the side of the game board acting as an opponent taking turns.

integrity.description: The agent played the game legitimately by flipping cards.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a progression of different cards being flipped and matched over time, rather than jumping instantly from a new board to a completed state.
